text:
Once upon a quiet autumn evening, nestled among whispering leaves, a tiny, worn-out hedgehog named Heddy prounced herself on the bed of dried grass and autumn leaves. Heddy had recently returned from an adventurous expedition through a forgotten forest where birds sang melodies only she could hear and mushrooms gleamed with secrets untouched by daylight.

She had embarked on this journey seeking the elusive Moonberry — a sweet berry known for its luminous glow that glowed brighter under the full moon. Heddy knew it could be found nowhere else but the heart of Wintercrest.

Today was no ordinary morning. Heddy's ears twitched as she heard a soft rustling. Out popped a friendly-looking fox with twinkling eyes. Before Heddy could even think, the fox spoke in a melodic voice, “Good morning, Miss Heddy! I’ve come across some Moonberries!”
